DISCOVERY 302, RE: PLAINTIFF GARY E. LOONEY, DBA COLLECTRONICS OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PLTF'S REQ FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S CASE WAS DISMISSED BY THE COURT ON APRIL 12, 2021. EVEN IF THE PLAINTIFF'S CASE WAS NOT DISMISSED, THE DEADLINES FOR DISCOVERY AND DISCOVERY MOTIONS HAVE LONG PASSED AS THE DATE INITIALLY SET FOR TRIAL IN THIS MATTER WAS APRIL 12, 2021. C.C.P. SEC. 2024.020. FAIRMONT INSURANCE COMPANY V. SUPERIOR COURT, 22 CAL.4TH 245, (2000); PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC., 165 CAL. APP. 4TH 1568 (2008); COTTINI V. ENLOE MED. CTR., 226 CAL. APP. 4TH 401 (2014). SINCE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF AGREEMENT AMONG THE PARTIES TO EXTEND DISCOVERY DEADLINES PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SEC. 2024.060 AND THERE IS NO PENDING OR CONCURRENT MOTION TO REOPEN/EXTEND DISCOVERY AND DISCOVERY MOTION DEADLINES PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SEC. 2024.050, THE COURT HAS NO CHOICE BUT TO DENY THE MOTION. PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC., 165 CAL. APP. 4TH 1568 (2008). THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM C. LYNN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Req For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF GARY E. LOONEY, DBA COLLECTRONICS OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Req For Production Of Docs.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion denied. Plaintiff's case was dismissed by the Court on April 12, 2021. Even if the Plaintiff's case was not dismissed, the deadlines for discovery and discovery motions have long passed as the date initially set for trial in this matter was April 12, 2021. C.C.P. Sec. 2024.020. Fairmont Insurance Company v. Superior Court, 22 Cal.4th 245, (2000); Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008); Cottini v. Enloe Med. Ctr., 226 Cal. App. 4th 401 (2014). Since there is no evidence of agreement among the parties to extend discovery deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.060 and there is no pending or concurrent motion to reopen/extend discovery and discovery motion deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.050, the Court has no choice but to deny the motion. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William C. Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF GARY E. LOONEY, DBA COLLECTRONICS OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant(s) shall, within 10 days, furnish responses to PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AND ADMISSION OF GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS.

Costs are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $90.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
